title: Dungeons & Dragons Basic/Expert (B/X)
resolution_core: d20 attack matrices vs. descending AC; d6 and 2d6 tables for exploration and reactions

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attack rolls are d20 against descending Armor Class, cross-referenced on class-based attack matrices; most non-combat tasks use separate d6 or 2d6 procedures instead of a unified DC.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.single_d6_table: Classic X-in-6 subsystems drive exploration: forcing stuck doors succeeds 2-in-6, hearing noise 1-in-6, and finding secret doors 1-in-6 (2-in-6 for elves).
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Seven classes define everything about a character, with race-as-class Dwarf, Elf, and Halfling standing alongside Cleric, Fighter, Magic-User, and Thief.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Ability scores are generated by rolling 3d6 for each of the six abilities, giving the 3-18 range that drives the compressed -3 to +3 modifier spread.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.stats_in_order: The 3d6 are rolled in fixed order Strength through Charisma; the only adjustment allowed is trading 2 points from certain scores for 1 point of prime requisite.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: Characters declare Law, Neutrality, or Chaos, a three-point axis that also grants secret alignment languages known only to like-aligned creatures.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Each class advances on its own XP table (a Magic-User needs 2,500 XP for level 2 versus the Thief's 1,200), and a high prime requisite grants a 5-10% XP bonus.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.treasure_as_x: Recovered treasure awards 1 XP per gold piece of value, so hauling loot out of the dungeon rather than fighting is the main advancement engine.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Five save categories - Death Ray/Poison, Magic Wands, Paralysis/Turn to Stone, Dragon Breath, and Rods/Staves/Spells - improve on class-specific tables as levels rise.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit dice vary by class (d4 for Magic-Users and Thieves, d8 for Fighters and Dwarves), and at 0 hit points a character is simply dead - there are no dying rules.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: The optional morale rule gives monsters a 2-12 score checked on 2d6 after their first casualty and at half strength; failure means flight or surrender.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.reaction_rolls: When monster motives are unclear the DM rolls 2d6 on the reaction table, adjusted by Charisma, with results running from immediate attack to friendship.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Initiative is a d6 rolled per side each round; the winning side moves and attacks first, and ties mean both sides act simultaneously.
vector_explanations.exploration.site.dungeon_turn_procedure: Dungeon exploration runs in 10-minute turns with movement rated in feet per turn, mandatory rest one turn in six, and time tracked against light and encounters.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.torch_economy: A torch burns for 6 turns and lights 30 feet; a lantern burns one oil flask per 24 turns, making light a consumable clock ticking over the whole delve.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.wandering_monster: The DM rolls 1d6 every two turns in the dungeon, with an encounter arriving on a 1, drawn from dungeon-level-keyed wandering monster tables.
vector_explanations.exploration.discovery.secret_doors: Searching a 10-foot area takes one full turn and reveals secret doors on a 1-in-6 (2-in-6 for elves), with only one attempt allowed per character per area.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.surprise_checks: Each side rolls 1d6 at the start of an encounter and is surprised on a 1-2, losing its actions for the first round while the other side acts freely.
vector_explanations.exploration.wilderness.hexcrawl_procedur: The Expert set formalizes wilderness travel on 24-mile hexes with daily movement by terrain type, chances of becoming lost, and per-hex encounter checks.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.weight_based: Encumbrance is measured in coins (cn) with 1,600 cn as the hard maximum, and growing loads step movement down through the 120'/90'/60'/30' bands.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.retainer_recruitment: Retainers are hired with an offer and a 2d6 reaction roll, capped in number by Charisma, and carry a loyalty score plus a half share of earned XP.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Magic-Users and Elves memorize specific spells from their spellbook each morning; a cast spell is erased from memory until it is memorized again.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells are ranked by level, with magic-user spells running 1st through 6th level and clerical spells 1st through 5th across the Basic and Expert sets.
vector_explanations.simulation.building.stronghold_construct: At name level (9th) the Expert rules price out castles and strongholds per wall, tower, and keep, letting PCs clear wilderness hexes and attract settlers.

title: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons (1st Edition)
resolution_core: d20 vs. class attack matrices and five save categories; percentile subsystems throughout

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attacks roll d20 against descending AC using class-specific combat matrices in the DMG (with the infamous repeating 20s) rather than a unified DC formula.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Race and class combine under strict limits: races cap advancement in most classes, and subclasses like Paladin, Ranger, Illusionist, Assassin, and Monk gate entry behind steep ability minimums.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: The DMG sanctions four generation methods, with Method I - roll 4d6, drop the lowest, arrange to taste - recommended for viable adventurers.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: The full nine-alignment grid (law/chaos crossed with good/evil) governs class eligibility, grants alignment languages, and punishes alignment change with lost levels.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Every class advances on its own XP table, and a prime requisite of 16 or more grants a 10% bonus to all experience earned.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.treasure_as_x: Treasure converts to experience at 1 XP per gold piece value, dwarfing monster XP and rewarding extraction over extermination.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.training_advancement: Leveling up requires formal training costing 1,500 gp per level per week, with the weeks needed set by the DM's rating of how well the character was played.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Five categories - paralyzation/poison/death magic, petrification/polymorph, rod/staff/wand, breath weapon, and spell - improve on class tables with level.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit dice run from the magic-user's d4 to the fighter's d10, stop accruing at name level in favor of flat bonuses, and exceptional Constitution adds up to +4 per die for fighters.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.weapon_speed: Weapon speed factors and casting segments refine timing within the round: a dagger (speed 2) can land before a two-handed sword (speed 10) when initiative ties.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Initiative is rolled on a d6 per side each round, then refined by weapon speed factors and the segment-based timing of spells and missile fire.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: The DMG assigns percentile base morale to monsters, henchmen, and hirelings, checked under fire and modified by casualties, leadership, and pay.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.reaction_rolls: Uncertain encounters use the DMG's percentile reaction table, adjusted by Charisma reaction bonuses, running from violent hostility to enthusiastic friendliness.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.surprise_checks: Each side rolls a d6 and is normally surprised on a 1-2, with the margin measured in segments during which the unsurprised side acts freely.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.wandering_monster: Wandering monsters are checked by die roll at intervals set per dungeon level or terrain, drawn from the DMG's extensive level-keyed encounter tables.
vector_explanations.exploration.site.random_dungeon_generati: Appendix A of the DMG generates entire dungeons procedurally - corridors, doors, chambers, treasure, and inhabitants - from cascading dice tables, usable for solo play.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.artifact_rules: The DMG details artifacts and relics like the Hand of Vecna whose powers are partly randomized per campaign and which carry terrible built-in costs.
vector_explanations.logistics.maintenance.hireling_upkeep: Henchmen are capped by Charisma and tracked with a percentile loyalty base, while hirelings from linkboys to sages demand listed monthly wages and upkeep.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.aging: Age categories from young adult to venerable adjust ability scores, and effects like a ghost's touch or casting certain spells age characters by years.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.resurrection_rules: Constitution sets percentile system shock and resurrection survival chances, and each body can only be raised a finite number of times tied to Constitution.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.wild_talents: Appendix I gives characters a small percentile chance of psionic wild talents, opening a full psionic combat subsystem of attack and defense modes.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Casters memorize spells from books or prayer after rest, with memorization time scaling by spell level, and each casting erases the spell from the mind.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.verbal_somatic_components: Every spell lists verbal, somatic, and material components, so a bound and gagged magic-user is helpless and material stocks must be tracked.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Magic-user spells span levels 1-9, clerics reach 7th, and illusionists and druids climb their own seven-level ladders at class-specific rates.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_lists_by_class: Magic-users, illusionists, clerics, and druids each own an exclusive spell list; illusionist magic in particular is unavailable to standard magic-users.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.spell_research: The DMG prices researching new spells and fabricating magic items in gold, weeks of work, and DM-supervised chance-of-failure rolls.
vector_explanations.stealth.vision.darkvision_senses: Demi-humans possess 60-foot infravision that reads heat rather than light and is spoiled by nearby torches and light spells.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.weight_based: Encumbrance is tallied in gold-piece weight units, with armor and treasure loads dropping base movement from 12 inches down toward 3.

title: Dungeons & Dragons 3.5
resolution_core: d20 + modifiers vs. Difficulty Class or Armor Class

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Everything unifies on d20 + modifiers versus a Difficulty Class or Armor Class - attacks, saves, skills, and opposed checks all share the same core roll.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Dexterity modifier rolled once at the start of combat, boostable by +4 with the Improved Initiative feat.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.full_round_actions: The full attack is a full-round action that unlocks iterative attacks - a +16 BAB fighter swings at +16/+11/+6/+1 - at the cost of moving more than a 5-foot step.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Leaving a threatened square or casting in melee provokes attacks of opportunity, limited to one per round unless you have Combat Reflexes.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.held_actions: Ready and delay let you hold a standard action against a stated trigger or voluntarily drop your initiative count, explicitly re-slotting the turn order.
vector_explanations.combat.movement.grid_based: Combat assumes a 5-foot square battle grid with diagonals costing 5 then 10 feet alternately and the 5-foot step as a non-provoking adjustment.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.square_grid: Miniature-scale rules define space, reach, and threatened squares per creature size category from Tiny to Colossal on the square grid.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.flanking: Melee attackers positioned on exactly opposite sides of a target gain +2 to hit and switch on the rogue's sneak attack dice.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Cover grants +4 AC and +2 on Reflex saves while concealment imposes a 20% or 50% miss chance, each with precise corner-to-corner line-drawing rules.
vector_explanations.spatial.geometry.area_of_effect_template: Spells use defined shapes - the fireball's 20-foot-radius spread, 15-foot cones, lines, and bursts - measured in grid squares from a point of origin.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Grappling chains a touch attack into opposed grapple checks (BAB + Strength + size modifier) with its own menu of restricted in-grapple actions.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.shove_trip: Bull rush and trip are opposed Strength and Dexterity contests with codified counter-trips, and feats like Improved Trip grant free follow-up attacks.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.charge: Charging requires 10+ feet of straight-line movement for +2 attack and -2 AC, doubles lance damage, and anchors Spirited Charge builds.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: A rules glossary defines exact conditions - dazed, entangled, nauseated, flat-footed, prone - each with specific action limits and numeric penalties.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.massive_damage: Taking 50 or more damage from a single blow forces a DC 15 Fortitude save; failure means instant death regardless of remaining hit points.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit points scale by class hit die from the wizard's d4 to the barbarian's d12 plus Constitution, with maximum hit points at 1st level.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you are disabled, from -1 to -9 you are dying and lose 1 hp per round with a 10% chance per round to stabilize, and at -10 you are dead.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_confirmation: A natural roll in the weapon's threat range (18-20 for a rapier) must be confirmed by a second attack roll before multiplying damage x2 to x4.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Fortitude, Reflex, and Will saves grow on good (+2 base) or poor (+0 base) class progressions and key off Constitution, Dexterity, and Wisdom.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Skill points per level (modified by Intelligence) buy ranks capped at character level +3, spread across a list of more than forty enumerated skills.
vector_explanations.character.skills.class_skill_lists: Each class defines class skills bought at 1 point per rank versus 2 points for cross-class skills, which also cap at half the normal maximum ranks.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Feats arrive at 1st level and every third level, forming build-defining chains like Power Attack into Cleave or Dodge into Spring Attack.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: The default generation method is 4d6 drop the lowest arranged freely, with point buy offered as a DMG variant for organized play.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: XP is awarded per encounter by Challenge Rating against party level and doubles as a spendable currency - crafting items and casting wish (5,000 XP) burn it.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: The nine-point alignment grid gates classes (paladins must be Lawful Good), powers spells like detect evil and holy word, and shifts with behavior.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Wizards and clerics prepare specific spells into their daily slots after rest; casting expends the preparation until the spell is prepared again.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Casters get slots per day per spell level from class tables, earn bonus slots for high ability scores, and pay metamagic costs by using higher-level slots.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.schools_of_magic: Wizards may specialize in one of eight schools for a bonus school slot per spell level at the price of two prohibited schools.
vector_explanations.magic.divine.domain_powers: Clerics choose two deity-appropriate domains, each granting a special power and one bonus domain spell per spell level.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells are graded 0 through 9th level, with caster level further scaling ranges, durations, and damage caps within each spell.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_lists_by_class: Sorcerer/wizard, cleric, druid, bard, paladin, and ranger each draw from distinct lists, which drive scroll, wand, and Use Magic Device compatibility.
vector_explanations.equipment.crafting.enchanting: Item creation feats like Craft Wondrous Item build magic items for half market price in gold plus one twenty-fifth of the price paid in XP.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_by_lev: The DMG's wealth-by-level table (49,000 gp at 10th level) calibrates PC equipment and NPC gear so encounter math stays balanced.
vector_explanations.social.persuasion.attitude_steps: Diplomacy shifts NPC attitudes along a fixed ladder - hostile, unfriendly, indifferent, friendly, helpful - against DCs set by the current step.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.strength_based: Carrying capacity derives from Strength in light/medium/heavy loads, with heavier loads capping Dexterity bonus, adding check penalties, and slowing speed.

title: Dungeons & Dragons (4th Edition)
resolution_core: d20 + half level + modifiers vs. AC/Fortitude/Reflex/Will defenses or level-tiered DCs

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: All checks are d20 + half level + modifiers; attacks target the static AC, Fortitude, Reflex, or Will defenses of the target instead of forcing saving throws.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each turn provides a standard, a move, and a minor action - downgradeable but never upgradeable - plus one immediate action per round.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.action_point_pools: Characters bank action points, gaining one at each two-encounter milestone, and may spend one per encounter to take an extra action.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Opportunity attacks punish leaving adjacent squares or ranged attacks in melee, while immediate interrupts and reactions trigger off enemy turns.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.square_grid: Combat is explicitly grid-required: powers count range and area in squares and creature size defines a footprint on the battlemat.
vector_explanations.combat.movement.grid_based: Movement is counted in squares, with shift (1 square without provoking), run (+2 squares but grant combat advantage), and difficult terrain costing extra.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.flanking: Flanking a target with an ally on the exact opposite side grants combat advantage (+2 to hit) and switches on rogue Sneak Attack.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Cover and superior cover impose -2 and -5 attack penalties respectively, with concealment mirroring them and corner-based sighting rules on the grid.
vector_explanations.spatial.movement.forced_displacement: Push, pull, and slide effects measured in squares are core power grammar - fighters push, wardens pull, and wizards slide enemies into hazards.
vector_explanations.spatial.geometry.area_of_effect_template: Powers use standardized burst and blast templates - close blast 3, area burst 1 within 10 - resolved directly in grid squares.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Standardized conditions like dazed, stunned, slowed, weakened, and marked mostly persist until a 'save ends' roll of 10 or higher at the end of your turn.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit points are generous and formulaic by class, and dropping to half total imposes the bloodied state that many powers and monsters key off.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.short_rest: A five-minute short rest lets characters spend healing surges (each worth a quarter of maximum hp, with surges per day set by class) and recharges encounter powers.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.long_rest: A six-hour extended rest restores all hit points, healing surges, and daily powers, and resets action points to one.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.recharge_powers: The AEDU structure gives every class at-will, encounter, and daily attack powers, while monsters carry recharge powers that return on a die roll such as recharge 5-6.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.at_will_cantrips: Every class has at-will attack powers usable every single round - the warlock's eldritch blast or the wizard's magic missile never run out.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Noncombat magic is spun off into rituals: castings of ten minutes or more with component costs in gold, learned from ritual books by any ritual caster.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Skill challenges require accumulating a set number of successes before three failures across varied skills, with complexity ratings scaling the target.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.tiered_difficu: The DMG supplies easy, moderate, and hard DC tables by character level, so difficulty numbers scale mechanically with the party's tier.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.tier_play: Play divides into heroic (1-10), paragon (11-20), and epic (21-30) tiers, layering paragon paths at 11th level and epic destinies at 21st.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Encounters are budgeted from monster XP values matched to party level, with quests granting fixed additional XP awards.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: The default is a 22-point purchase with escalating costs for high scores, alongside sanctioned standard arrays and dice rolling as variants.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you fall unconscious and roll a death saving throw each round: three failures before a rest kill you, and a natural 20 lets you spend a surge to rally.
vector_explanations.character.skills.proficiency_binary: Skills are simply trained or untrained - training grants a flat +5 - and everyone adds half their level to every skill check.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Feats arrive at 1st level and every even level (plus 11th and 21st), drawn from tier-gated heroic, paragon, and epic feat lists.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Dexterity modifier + half level, rolled once at the start of the encounter to fix the turn order.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.charge: Charge is a standard action moving at least 2 squares in a line for a +1 bonus melee basic attack, normally ending your turn.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.shove_trip: Bull rush is a Strength attack versus Fortitude that pushes the target a square, and many martial powers knock prone or slide targets as riders.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: The grab action is a Strength attack versus Reflex that immobilizes the target until it escapes with Acrobatics or Athletics against fixed escape DCs.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_by_lev: Treasure parcels prescribe exactly how many magic items of which levels and how much gold a party should receive across each level of play.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Classes are built on role (defender, striker, leader, controller) and power source (martial, arcane, divine), structuring party composition and power lists.

title: Dungeons & Dragons (5th Edition)
resolution_core: d20 + ability modifier + proficiency vs. DC or AC, with advantage/disadvantage

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: The core d20 test adds an ability modifier and proficiency bonus against a DC or AC, with bounded accuracy keeping targets in a narrow 10-30 band.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.advantage_disadvant: Situational modifiers collapse into advantage and disadvantage - roll two d20s and keep the higher or lower - which never stack beyond a single instance.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.inspiration: The DM awards Inspiration for playing to personality traits, ideals, bonds, and flaws; a player spends it for advantage on one roll of their choice.
vector_explanations.character.skills.proficiency_binary: A single proficiency bonus (+2 to +6 by level) applies fully or not at all to skills, saves, tools, and attacks - there are no skill ranks.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Backgrounds like Soldier or Sage grant two skill proficiencies, equipment, and a roleplaying feature, anchoring the character's pre-adventuring life.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.standard_array: Ability scores default to the 15/14/13/12/10/8 standard array, with 27-point buy and 4d6-drop-lowest rolling as sanctioned alternatives.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Twelve classes each branch into subclasses - arcane traditions, divine domains, martial archetypes - chosen by 3rd level that define the build.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you roll death saving throws against DC 10: three successes stabilize, three failures kill, a natural 20 restores 1 hp, and massive damage kills outright.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.short_rest: A one-hour short rest lets characters spend Hit Dice to heal and refreshes short-rest resources like warlock pact slots, ki points, and Channel Divinity.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.long_rest: An eight-hour long rest restores all hit points, half of maximum Hit Dice, and all spell slots, and is capped at one per 24 hours.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.concentration_limits: Many ongoing spells require concentration: only one at a time, broken by starting another concentration spell or failing a DC 10+ Constitution save when damaged.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Spells with the ritual tag can be cast in ten extra minutes without burning a slot; wizards can ritual-cast directly from the spellbook without preparing.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.at_will_cantrips: Cantrips are unlimited-use 0-level spells whose damage scales at character levels 5, 11, and 17, so casters are never fully out of magic.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Casting consumes slots by level, upcasting through a higher slot amplifies many spells, and all full casters share a single slot progression table.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.pact_granted: Warlocks draw magic from a patron pact, casting through a tiny pool of slots that all refresh on a short rest, supplemented by eldritch invocations.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.counterspelling: Counterspell is a reaction that automatically cancels spells of 3rd level or lower and needs an ability check against DC 10 + spell level for anything higher.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.wild_magic_surges: The Wild Magic sorcerer rolls on a d100 surge table - from a self-centered fireball to becoming a potted plant - when the DM invokes a surge after a casting.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.magic_item_attunement: Powerful magic items require attunement performed over a short rest, with a hard cap of three attuned items per character.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.item_rarity: Magic items are graded common through legendary, with rarity setting suggested prices and the character levels at which items are appropriate.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Appendix A defines exact conditions - grappled, restrained, frightened, invisible, exhaustion - that spells and monster abilities reference by name.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.exhaustion: Exhaustion stacks in six levels of escalating penalties, from disadvantage on ability checks at level one to death at level six; a long rest removes one level.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Each round grants one reaction, spent on opportunity attacks against creatures leaving your reach or on triggered spells like shield and counterspell.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Grappling and shoving replace an attack with an Athletics contest against Athletics or Acrobatics, reducing speed to 0 or knocking the target prone.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.theater_of_mind: The core rules run range and movement narratively in theater of the mind, with the 5-foot grid explicitly labeled an optional variant in the DMG.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is a straight Dexterity check rolled once at the start of combat, fixing the turn order for the whole encounter.
vector_explanations.stealth.detection.passive_perception: Passive Perception (10 + Perception modifier) sets the threshold that hiding creatures must beat and catches unnoticed threats without any roll.
vector_explanations.stealth.vision.darkvision_senses: Darkvision - usually 60 feet, treating darkness as dim light - is widespread among ancestries and interacts with the light and obscurement rules.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.group_checks: Group ability checks succeed when at least half the party beats the DC, smoothing over weak links during group stealth or survival tasks.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.milestone_advanceme: The DMG sanctions milestone or story-based advancement, leveling the whole party at narrative beats instead of tallying individual XP.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Monsters carry XP values by Challenge Rating, and the DMG budgets encounters with per-character XP thresholds and multipliers for monster count.
vector_explanations.magic.divine.divine_intervention_pleas: Clerics of 10th level and up can plead for Divine Intervention: roll percentile dice at or under cleric level and the deity acts directly.
vector_explanations.downtime.projects.spell_transcription: Wizards copy new spells into their spellbook at 2 hours and 50 gp per spell level, turning captured scrolls and enemy spellbooks into treasure.

title: Pathfinder Roleplaying Game (1st Edition)
resolution_core: d20 + modifiers vs. DC, AC, or Combat Maneuver Defense

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: The unified d20 + modifiers roll targets DCs, Armor Class, or the new Combat Maneuver Defense, refining the 3.5 chassis it is built on.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_confirmation: Rolls in the weapon's threat range (a keen falchion threatens on 15-20) need a confirmation roll before applying x2 to x4 multipliers, and critical feats key off confirmed crits.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Dexterity rolled once at combat start, with ties broken by initiative modifier and Improved Initiative granting +4.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.full_round_actions: Full attacks grant iterative attacks at descending -5 steps of Base Attack Bonus, forcing the classic choice between standing to swing and moving.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Movement, ranged attacks, and casting in threatened squares provoke attacks of opportunity, with Combat Reflexes adding Dexterity-based extra AoOs.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.held_actions: Ready sets a standard action against a declared trigger and delay voluntarily lowers your count, both explicitly re-slotting your place in initiative.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.square_grid: Reach, size categories from Fine to Colossal, and threatened areas are all defined in 5-foot squares for miniatures play.
vector_explanations.combat.movement.grid_based: Tactical movement uses the grid with 5-foot steps, difficult terrain, and diagonal costs alternating 5 and 10 feet.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.flanking: Opposite-side melee attackers gain +2 to hit and enable sneak attack, with teamwork feats manipulating flanking geometry further.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Cover gives +4 AC and +2 Reflex, soft cover blocks attacks of opportunity, and concealment applies a 20% or 50% miss chance.
vector_explanations.spatial.geometry.area_of_effect_template: Bursts, cones, lines, and spreads are measured in squares from an origin intersection, with the fireball's 20-foot-radius spread as the archetype.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: The streamlined maneuver system resolves grapples as a single CMB check (BAB + Strength + size) against the target's CMD, replacing 3.5's opposed-check ladder.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.disarm: Disarm is a combat maneuver check against CMD that provokes unless you have Improved Disarm, knocking the weapon away on success.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.shove_trip: Trip, bull rush, overrun, and drag each resolve as CMB versus CMD, with feat chains adding bonuses and removing the provoked attack.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.charge: Charging in a straight line gives +2 to hit and -2 AC and pairs with lances and Spirited Charge for doubled or tripled damage.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: An appendix codifies conditions like staggered, entangled, shaken, and nauseated, each with exact penalties referenced throughout the rules.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Class hit dice run d6 (wizard) to d12 (barbarian), and favored class bonuses let characters take +1 hp or +1 skill rank every level.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Characters die at negative hit points equal to Constitution score rather than a flat -10, are staggered at exactly 0, and stabilize on a Constitution check.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Fortitude, Reflex, and Will progress on good (+2 base) or poor (+0 base) class tracks keyed to Constitution, Dexterity, and Wisdom.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Skills are consolidated (Perception folds together Spot, Listen, and Search) and bought at one rank per point with ranks capped by Hit Dice.
vector_explanations.character.skills.class_skill_lists: Class skills grant a flat +3 bonus once a single rank is invested, replacing 3.5's cross-class half-rank bookkeeping entirely.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Feats arrive at every odd level - twice the 3.5 rate - feeding long chains like Power Attack, Furious Focus, and Dreadful Carnage.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: The Core Rulebook's purchase system prices ability scores from 10, 15, 20, or 25-point budgets by campaign power level, alongside classic rolling methods.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Encounter XP is read from Challenge Rating tables against slow, medium, or fast advancement tracks, letting tables tune their leveling speed.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: The nine-alignment grid persists with class requirements - monks lawful, barbarians nonlawful - and alignment-keyed spells and channeling.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Casters gain slots per day by class table with bonus slots for high casting stats; sorcerers and bards cast spontaneously from spells known.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Wizards and clerics prepare specific spells into daily slots after rest; a spell not prepared that morning simply cannot be cast that day.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.schools_of_magic: Wizard arcane schools grant active school powers such as evocation's force missile plus a bonus school slot per level, offset by two opposition schools.
vector_explanations.magic.divine.domain_powers: Cleric domains each grant granted powers at 1st and 8th level plus a domain slot per spell level filled from the domain's fixed list.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.bloodline_innate: Sorcerer bloodlines - draconic, abyssal, arcane, and more - add bloodline arcana, bonus spells, and powers at fixed levels as inherited magic.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells rank 0 through 9th, and this edition makes 0-level cantrips and orisons at-will castings while caster level still scales effects.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_lists_by_class: Each casting class owns its list (wizard/sorcerer, cleric, druid, bard, paladin, ranger), governing scroll use and Use Magic Device checks.
vector_explanations.equipment.crafting.enchanting: Item creation feats craft items at half market price in gold - Pathfinder drops 3.5's XP costs - against Spellcraft DCs of 5 + caster level.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_by_lev: The wealth-by-level table (62,000 gp at 10th) and NPC gear budgets calibrate loot drops so character power tracks the encounter math.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Eleven core classes with heavily expanded features - rage powers, rogue talents, bloodlines, domains - keep single-class play rewarding through 20th level.

title: Pathfinder (2nd Edition)
resolution_core: d20 + proficiency vs. DC with four degrees of success (beat or miss the DC by 10 for criticals)

vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.three_action_syste: Every turn is three interchangeable actions plus one reaction; Striding, Striking, and casting (most spells cost two actions) all draw from the same pool.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.multiple_action_pe: Successive attacks in one turn suffer the multiple attack penalty of -5 then -10, reduced to -4/-8 by weapons with the agile trait.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Each round grants one reaction with a stated trigger; notably, Attack of Opportunity is a fighter feature and monster ability rather than a universal rule.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.degrees_of_success: Every check grades four outcomes - beat the DC by 10 for critical success, miss by 10 for critical failure - and spells, saves, and maneuvers define effects for all four tiers.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: A natural 20 steps the outcome up one degree and a natural 1 steps it down, so criticals emerge from the degree ladder rather than a flat threat range.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.hero_points: The GM hands out Hero Points (one at session start, more for heroics); spend one to reroll a check or all of them to avoid death outright.
vector_explanations.character.identity.ancestry_heritage: Characters pick an ancestry, then a heritage within it (versatile human, cavern elf), and continue taking ancestry feats at 1st, 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th levels.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Backgrounds grant two ability boosts, a skill, a lore skill, and a skill feat, welding backstory into the Ancestry-Background-Class build sequence.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Classes advance through chains of class feats chosen at even levels, so two fighters with different feat picks play completely differently.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Proficiency runs untrained, trained, expert, master, legendary - adding level plus 2/4/6/8 - and gates rank-locked skill feats like Cloud Jump.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Nearly every level grants a feat from a defined pool - ancestry, class, skill, or general - making feat selection the core loop of advancement.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Conditions are standardized and often valued - frightened 2, clumsy 1, doomed 1 - with explicit rules for how each value decrements.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you gain dying 1 (dying 2 from a critical), make recovery checks against DC 10 + dying value, and accrue the wounded condition with each rescue.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.shield_sacrifice: Shield Block is a reaction that subtracts the shield's Hardness from incoming damage while shield and wielder split the rest - shields dent and break saving your life.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.item_rarity: Every item, spell, and creature carries a rarity - common, uncommon, rare, unique - controlling default player access and GM gating.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.enchantment_item_craftin: Weapon and armor potency and property runes are etched onto gear and can be transferred between items, forming the backbone of the magic-item economy.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.abstract_load_leve: Encumbrance uses Bulk: light items count as L, ten L equal 1 Bulk, and you become encumbered when carrying more than 5 + Strength modifier Bulk.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.at_will_cantrips: Cantrips are unlimited and automatically heighten to half your level rounded up, so blasting chassis scale without any slot expenditure.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Prepared and spontaneous casters alike track slots per spell rank, and spontaneous casters need signature spells to heighten a spell freely.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.recharge_powers: Focus spells cost Focus Points from a pool capped at three that refills with a ten-minute Refocus activity, giving classes rechargeable signature magic.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Rituals are hour-long, multi-participant castings where primary and secondary casters make skill checks, and even martial characters can serve as casters.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells span ranks 1 through 10 and heightening is explicit: casting from a higher slot upgrades listed effects, replacing caster-level scaling.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.tiered_difficu: A DC-by-level table (15 at level 1 up to 40 at level 20) plus simple DCs by proficiency rank standardize difficulty across the whole game.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.square_grid: Combat assumes 5-foot squares with size, reach, and line-of-effect rules written for the grid.
vector_explanations.combat.movement.grid_based: Stride, Step (5 feet without triggering reactions), and difficult terrain resolve on the grid with diagonals alternating 5 and 10 feet.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.flanking: Flanking makes the target flat-footed (-2 AC) against the flankers, serving as the primary engine of rogue sneak attack.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Lesser, standard, and greater cover grant +1/+2/+4 circumstance bonuses to AC and Reflex, and the Take Cover action upgrades the tier.
vector_explanations.spatial.geometry.area_of_effect_template: Bursts, cones, emanations, and lines are defined grid templates; fireball is a 20-foot burst with a basic Reflex save graded by degree of success.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Grapple is an Athletics check against Fortitude DC: success grabs, critical success restrains, and critical failure can leave you prone or grabbed instead.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.shove_trip: Shove and Trip are Athletics maneuvers against Fortitude and Reflex DCs, graded by degrees so criticals push farther or add damage.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.disarm: Disarm is an Athletics check against Reflex DC - a critical success knocks the weapon free while a plain success only loosens the target's grip.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.perception_based: Initiative is normally a Perception check, but the GM can call for Stealth or Deception (Avoid Notice), tying initiative to how you approached the fight.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.day_job_rolls: The Earn Income downtime activity rolls a skill against a task-level DC to bank days of pay from a table graded by degree of success.
vector_explanations.social.persuasion.attitude_steps: NPC attitudes are conditions on a five-step ladder from hostile to helpful, and Make an Impression or Request actions move and exploit those steps.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Leveling always costs a flat 1,000 XP, and encounters award XP by creature level relative to the party within trivial-to-extreme threat budgets.

title: 13th Age
resolution_core: d20 + ability modifier + level vs. AC/PD/MD or tiered DCs, with a rising escalation die

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attacks and checks roll d20 + ability modifier + level against AC, Physical Defense, Mental Defense, or environment DCs of 15/20/25 at adventurer tier.
vector_explanations.meta.structure_tools.escalation_die: The escalation die starts at 0 and ticks up each combat round, adding its value to all PC attack rolls and unlocking abilities that trigger when it runs high.
vector_explanations.social.relationships.relationship_dice: Each PC spreads 3 points among the setting's thirteen icons; rolling those relationship dice for 5s and 6s earns helpful or complicated icon interventions.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.fact_declaration: Every character's One Unique Thing is a player-authored true fact about the world - 'the lich king's only escaped apprentice' - that shapes the campaign without granting combat power.
vector_explanations.character.skills.freeform_skills: There is no skill list: players invent backgrounds like 'ex-imperial legion scout +4' and add the rating whenever the background plausibly applies to a check.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Classes range from the deliberately simple barbarian to the intricate wizard, each combining talents, powers, and tiered feats chosen at level-up.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Abilities are rolled 4d6 drop lowest (or point-bought to match), after which race and class each add +2 to two different scores.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.milestone_advanceme: Characters level after roughly three to four full heal-ups, with incremental advances handing out one piece of the next level between full level-ups.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.die_by_class: Weapon damage dice are set by class rather than weapon - a rogue deals d8s with any light blade - and you roll one weapon die per character level.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.zones_abstract: Positioning is abstract: combatants are engaged, nearby, or far away, with intercepting and disengage checks replacing grid movement entirely.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.recharge_powers: Many powers carry recharge values - roll 11+ or 16+ after the battle to regain them - layered on top of the at-will, once-per-battle, and daily usage tiers.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.at_will_cantrips: Wizards sling at-will attack spells and utility cantrips every round, so even slotless turns still produce magic.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Casters can burn a prepared spell to improvise a ritual, taking minutes to hours with effects negotiated with the GM rather than fixed text.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.short_rest: Quick rests after each battle let characters spend recoveries, rolling class recovery dice (like 3d8 + Constitution at 3rd level) to heal between fights.
vector_explanations.combat.restoration.long_rest: A full heal-up restores hit points, recoveries, and daily powers, and by default arrives after roughly four battles rather than by the clock.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit points scale from a class base value multiplied by level-based factors, and dropping to half total makes you staggered, which many abilities key off.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you fall and roll death saves: 16+ lets you spend a recovery and rejoin the fight, while four failures before help arrives mean death.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Dexterity modifier + level rolled once per battle, with monster initiative interleaved among the PCs' results.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.fail_forward: The skill chapter bakes in fail forward: failed checks are meant to produce progress with complications rather than stalled scenes.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Conditions like dazed, weakened, and stuck use the save-ends framework: roll 11+ at end of turn (6+ for easy saves, 16+ for hard) to shake them off.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Feats come in adventurer, champion, and epic tiers, and most upgrade a specific class power or talent rather than granting generic bonuses.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.tier_play: Levels 1-10 split into adventurer, champion, and epic tiers that gate feat tiers, raise skill DCs, and escalate icon-level threats.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.sentient_items: True magic items have quirks and personalities - a bold cloak makes you boastful - and carrying more items than your level lets their quirks take over your behavior.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells and powers come in odd-numbered levels, and at level-up casters swap lower-level spells for higher-level versions of their slots.

title: Shadow of the Demon Lord
resolution_core: d20 + attribute modifier ± boon/bane d6s vs. 10 (challenge rolls) or target Defense

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attack rolls target the defender's Defense or an opposing attribute while challenge rolls always target a flat 10, with difficulty expressed through boons and banes instead of moving DCs.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.boost_setback_dice: Boons and banes are d6s attached by circumstance: roll them all, add the single highest to (or subtract it from) the d20, and equal numbers of boons and banes cancel out.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Characters stack a novice path at level 1, an expert path at 3, and a master path at 7 - Magician into Wizard into Diviner - instead of one fixed class.
vector_explanations.character.skills.freeform_skills: There is no skill list: professions like gravedigger or courtier are freeform tags that justify boons or automatic success on rolls where they plausibly apply.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.random_back: Each ancestry chapter is packed with roll tables - age, build, appearance, personality, and often grim background events - to randomize a level-0 start.
vector_explanations.character.identity.ancestry_heritage: Ancestries (human, changeling, clockwork, dwarf, goblin, orc) set starting attribute profiles and each grants a signature ancestry trait at level 4.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.corruption: Casting spells from Forbidden traditions and committing atrocities add Corruption points, which attract marks of darkness and doom the soul of anyone who dies corrupted.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_meter: Insanity accumulates from horror and dark magic on a track capped by Will, receding only slowly and marking how close the mind is to breaking.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.horror_checks: Witnessing horrifying scenes forces a Will challenge roll; failure inflicts Insanity, with truly cosmic horrors dealing multiple points at once.
vector_explanations.actions.timing.turn_order_selection: Each round players choose fast or slow turns: fast turns act before the enemy but allow only a move or an action, while slow turns act later but allow both.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.schools_of_magic: Magic splits into dozens of traditions - Air, Battle, Necromancy, Forbidden - and casters build repertoires by discovering traditions and learning their spells.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Each spell is cast a set number of times per rest based on your Power score and the spell's rank, rather than drawing from one shared slot pool.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells are ranked 0 through 10, a caster's Power score gates the highest rank learnable, and rank-0 spells can be cast most often.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.level_up_spell_picks: Paths grant 'discover a tradition or learn a spell' picks at set levels, so magical repertoires grow on a fixed, predictable schedule.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.milestone_advanceme: The whole group levels together after completing each adventure, taking characters from level 0 to 10 across roughly eleven sessions.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.campaign_arcs: The game is structured as a campaign arc toward the Demon Lord's shadow falling on the world, with starting, expert, and master tiers escalating to an apocalyptic finale.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Health derives from Strength plus path increases; damage accumulates upward and you fall when damage equals Health, with Healing Rate metering recovery.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Damage equal to Health leaves you incapacitated and making end-of-round fate rolls where an unlucky result kills; healing below the threshold revives you.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Afflictions such as dazed, frightened, immobilized, and poisoned are keyworded states referenced consistently by spells, talents, and monster attacks.
vector_explanations.downtime.recovery.natural_healing_rates: A full night's rest heals your Healing Rate - one quarter of your Health - so natural recovery is slow without magic or dedicated care.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.madness_tables: When Insanity reaches your Will you go mad, rolling on a table of screaming fits, fugues, and phobias, and repeated breaks can leave permanent quirks.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.derived_attributes: Defense, Health, Perception, Speed, and Power all derive from the four core attributes and path choices - Perception starts equal to Intellect, Defense to Agility.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.armor_types: Armor grades from soft leather to plate either set a fixed base Defense or add a small bonus, trading cost and Strength requirements for protection.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.weapon_properties_tags: Weapons carry traits - cumbersome, defensive, finesse, reach, thrown - that add boons or banes and determine who can wield them effectively.

title: Dungeon Crawl Classics Role Playing Game
resolution_core: d20 (or a dice-chain die) + modifiers vs. AC/DC, with Luck bending the odds

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: The core roll is d20 + modifiers versus AC or DC in classic d20 fashion, but which die you actually roll can slide up or down the dice chain.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.step_dice: The dice chain (d3-d4-d5-d6-d7-d8-d10-d12-d14-d16-d20-d24-d30) steps action dice up or down: untrained tasks roll a d10 and off-hand attacks step down.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.luck_stat: Luck is a full ability score with a rolled birth augur that permanently ties its modifier to one roll type, such as all attack rolls or all spell checks.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.luck_burn: Any character can permanently burn Luck points one-for-one as bonuses to a roll; thieves and halflings burn it at better rates and regenerate it overnight.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.funnel_play: Play begins as a funnel of 2-4 zero-level peasants per player; the survivors of that meat-grinder first adventure choose a class at level 1.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Abilities are rolled on 3d6 straight, feeding the funnel's disposable-peasant math where hopeless scores are part of the fun.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.stats_in_order: Scores stand exactly where they land, in order from Strength to Luck, with no rearranging allowed at generation.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Classes are Warrior, Wizard, Cleric, and Thief plus race-as-class Dwarf, Elf, and Halfling, each with signature engines like the warrior's deed die.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.random_back: A d100 occupation table assigns every zero-level a trade, a trained weapon, and trade goods - from gongfarmer with a trowel to turnip farmer with a hen.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.skill_roll_casting: Spells are cast with a spell check of d20 + caster level + ability modifier read on a per-spell result table where higher totals produce spectacular effects.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.mishap_tables: Botched castings trigger per-spell misfire tables, so a failed fireball has its own bespoke catalog of disasters distinct from any other spell.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.corruption: Wizard corruption from natural 1s is permanent and physical: minor, major, and greater corruption tables twist flesh with weeping sores or tentacled limbs.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.pact_granted: Wizards and elves can bond with supernatural patrons, gaining invoke patron castings, patron taint, and patron-specific spells at the price of service.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.attribute_burn_casting: Spellburn lets casters sacrifice points of Strength, Agility, or Stamina before a spell check for +1 each, recovering them only through rest and quests.
vector_explanations.magic.divine.sin_and_atonement: Cleric failures accumulate deity disapproval, widening the natural-roll range that angers the god; penance and sacrifice shrink the range back down.
vector_explanations.magic.divine.divine_intervention_pleas: Clerics can beg raw divine aid beyond their spell list, at the immediate price of jumping their disapproval range by ten points.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.fumble_tables: Natural 1s roll on fumble tables with the fumble die set by armor - plate rolls a d16 while the unarmored roll a d4 - modified by Luck.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: Natural crits roll on class-and-level-keyed critical hit tables (I through V) with the crit die scaling from d4 up to d30 for high-level warriors.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.combat_stunts: Warriors and dwarves attach a Mighty Deed of Arms to each attack: the deed die (starting at d3) adds to attack and damage, and a 3+ makes the declared stunt happen.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: XP arrives in grants of 0-4 per encounter based on danger survived, with tiny thresholds like 10 XP to reach 1st level keeping the ladder tight.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Zero-levels get 1d4 hit points; classed characters roll class hit dice each level, from the wizard's d4 up to the warrior's d12.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Agility modifier rolled individually, warriors add their class level, and two-handed weapons force a d16 initiative die.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Wizard and cleric spells run levels 1 through 5, and each spell's check table scales results from abject failure up to world-shaking manifestations.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: Law, Neutrality, and Chaos define which gods and patrons a caster may serve and what counts as unholy for a cleric's turning ability.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp a character dies unless an ally recovers the body quickly; a successful Luck check reveals they were merely left for dead, alive but permanently scarred with lost Stamina.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.armor_types: Armor grants AC alongside a check penalty and a fumble die: heavier armor steps the fumble die up the chain and slows speed, making plate a real tradeoff.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Fortitude, Reflex, and Willpower saves progress on class tables and key off Stamina, Agility, and Personality respectively.

title: Old-School Essentials
resolution_core: d20 attack matrices (with an official ascending-AC option) plus X-in-6 and 2d6 subsystems

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attack rolls use the classic descending-AC matrices, with ascending AC and attack bonuses presented as a parallel official option throughout the text.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.single_d6_table: The B/X X-in-6 engine is preserved intact: force doors 2-in-6, hear noise 1-in-6, and search out secret doors 1-in-6 (2-in-6 for elves).
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Classic Fantasy restates the seven B/X classes, including race-as-class Dwarf, Elf, and Halfling, in OSE's terse one-spread-per-topic reference format.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Ability scores are 3d6 down the line, producing modifiers in the compressed B/X range of -3 to +3.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: Characters and monsters align with Law, Neutrality, or Chaos, and the alignment languages of B/X carry over unchanged.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Each class levels on its own XP table, with prime requisite modifiers of -20% to +10% applied to every award.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.treasure_as_x: One gold piece of recovered treasure equals 1 XP, which the rules note will typically account for three quarters or more of all XP earned.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: The five classic categories - death, wands, paralysis, breath, spells - improve on per-class tables, printed cleanly on each class spread.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit dice run from the magic-user's and thief's d4 to the fighter's and dwarf's d8, and 0 hit points means death.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: Monsters carry a 2-12 morale rating checked on 2d6 at first blood and at half strength; passing twice means they fight to the end.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.reaction_rolls: Unplanned encounters roll 2d6 on the reaction table, modified by Charisma and circumstances, from hostile attack through friendly welcome.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Each side rolls 1d6 per round, and the winning side resolves its whole phase sequence - movement, missiles, magic, melee - first.
vector_explanations.exploration.site.dungeon_turn_procedure: The codified dungeon turn: parties move at their exploration rate per 10-minute turn, the referee checks encounters and light, and rest is required one turn in six.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.torch_economy: Torches last six turns and lanterns 24 turns per oil flask, each lighting 30 feet, so illumination is a strict consumable budget.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.wandering_monster: The referee rolls 1d6 at a set frequency (typically every second turn); a 1 brings a wandering monster from the dungeon level's table.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.encounter_distanc: Random encounters begin at 2d6 x 10 feet in dungeons, adjusted by surprise, giving both sides room to parley, posture, or flee.
vector_explanations.exploration.site.room_stocking: The referee stocks keyed maps with the B/X procedure: roll per room for monster, special, trap, or empty, each with its own chance of treasure.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.weight_based: Optional coin-based encumbrance tracks load in coins against a 1,600-coin ceiling, stepping movement through the 120'/90'/60'/30' bands.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.retainer_recruitment: Retainers are courted with an offer and a 2d6 reaction roll, limited in number by Charisma, with loyalty ratings tested when things turn grim.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Arcane and divine casters memorize spells per day from class tables, and casting erases the spell until it is memorized again.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Magic-user spells span six levels and cleric spells five, presented one spell per entry in OSE's quick-reference layout.
vector_explanations.exploration.wilderness.hexcrawl_procedur: Overland travel converts movement rates to miles per day with terrain modifiers, chances of getting lost, and per-terrain encounter tables for hexcrawling.

title: Shadowdark RPG
resolution_core: d20 + stat modifier vs. DC 9/12/15/18 or AC, with advantage/disadvantage

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Everything is d20 + stat modifier against DC 9, 12, 15, or 18 (easy through extreme) or against AC, with no skill list mediating the roll.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.advantage_disadvant: Advantage and disadvantage - roll two d20s and keep the better or worse - are the universal situational modifier, borrowed from 5e and applied everywhere.
vector_explanations.meta.real_time.real_time_timers: Torches burn for one hour of real time at the table, so the session clock itself is a survival mechanic the GM tracks with a literal timer.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.light_sources_timers: Light is the core resource: torches, lanterns, and light spells all have explicit burn durations, and losing your last flame in the deep is a crisis.
vector_explanations.stealth.vision.light_levels: Player characters have no darkvision; in total darkness you are effectively blind, so the radius of a held light source defines what you can see and fight.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.slot_based_invento: Gear slots equal your Strength score (minimum ten); every item fills slots, making carrying capacity a visible, hard-capped grid on the sheet.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Stats are 3d6 in order; if the set contains no score of 14 or higher you may scrap it and reroll all six from scratch.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.stats_in_order: The six scores land in order from Strength to Charisma, so the dice suggest your class rather than the other way around.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Four classes - fighter, priest, thief, wizard - carry distinct hit dice, weapon permissions, and randomly rolled class talents.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.random_back: A d20 background table (urchin, cult initiate, ranger and more) plus random name tables seed a quick backstory hook at creation.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.skill_roll_casting: Casting is a check of d20 + Intelligence or Wisdom against DC 10 + spell tier; on a failure the spell is lost until you rest, keeping magic tense and rationed.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.mishap_tables: A natural 1 while casting is a catastrophe: wizards roll on escalating mishap tables for backfires, while priests must atone before the lost spell returns.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hp you fall dying and roll a death timer of d4 + Constitution modifier rounds (minimum 1); allies must stabilize or heal you before it expires.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.treasure_as_x: XP comes almost entirely from hauling treasure back to safety, with finds rated from a few XP for minor valuables up to ten for legendary prizes.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.carousing_tables: Spending gold on carousing between delves rolls on a table of windfalls and disasters, converting the spree into bonus XP and complications.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Everyone rolls d20 + Dexterity once; the highest result acts first and play then proceeds clockwise around the real table from that person.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: Monsters check morale with a DC 15 Wisdom check when reduced to half strength or when half their group falls; failure sends them fleeing.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.wandering_monster: The GM rolls random encounter checks at regular crawling-round intervals, keeping constant pressure on parties who dawdle in the dark.
vector_explanations.exploration.site.dungeon_turn_procedure: Exploration runs in crawling rounds where each PC moves and takes an action while the GM advances dangers, checks encounters, and burns down light timers.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Class talents are rolled on a 2d6 table rather than chosen, granting semi-random boons like weapon mastery or improved spellcasting as you level.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.reroll_resources: Luck tokens handed out by the GM for clever or bold play can be spent to reroll any roll you just made, then they are gone.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit points are rolled on small class hit dice each level (plus Constitution modifier), staying low enough that every torch-lit fight is dangerous.

title: Worlds Without Number
resolution_core: 2d6 + skill + attribute vs. difficulty 6-12 for skills; d20 + attack bonus vs. AC for combat

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.2d6_plus_skill: Skill checks are 2d6 + skill level + attribute modifier against difficulties from 6 (easy) to 12 (heroic), with untrained attempts rolling at a penalty.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Combat runs on d20 + attack bonus + attribute versus ascending AC, deliberately pairing an OSR combat engine with the 2d6 skill system.
vector_explanations.simulation.faction.faction_turns: Factions get statblocks (Cunning, Force, Wealth) and assets, and act on scheduled faction turns to expand, attack rivals, and reshape the sandbox off-screen.
vector_explanations.exploration.wilderness.region_generation: GM chapters procedurally generate regions, communities, courts, and ruins from tag tables, building the sandbox one structured roll at a time.
vector_explanations.campaign.management.adventure_hooks_tabl: Dozens of adventure-seed tags - blighted land, cursed nobility - each come with pre-built enemies, friends, complications, things, and places to hook adventures.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Characters pick Warrior, Expert, or Mage, or build the hybrid Adventurer from two partial classes, layered with backgrounds and foci.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.random_back: Backgrounds like Peasant or Noble can be resolved by rolling on their Growth and Learning tables or by taking the fixed skill picks.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Skills rate from 0 to 4 with level-based caps, are bought with skill points, and add directly to the 2d6 roll.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Foci are feat-like talents such as Alert or Polymath taken at fixed levels, each with two ranks granting bonus skills and special abilities.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Attributes are 3d6 in order with the option to set any one score to 14 afterward, or you may take a standard array instead.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: Creature statblocks carry a Morale score checked on 2d6 when a fight turns against them; failure means routing, fleeing, or surrender.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit dice are d6-based by class; at each level you reroll your entire hit die pool and keep the new total if it is higher than before.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Zero hit points means mortally wounded: without rapid stabilization with the Heal skill or magical aid within a few rounds, the character dies.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is 1d8 + Dexterity modifier rolled individually at the start of combat, with ties resolved in the players' favor.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Turns provide a Main Action, a Move, and free On Turn instants, with the option to trade the Main Action for a second Move.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Mages prepare spells from those they know and cast only a handful per day by level - a deliberately harsh, low-slot Vancian economy.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.level_up_spell_picks: Mages add new spells from their tradition's list when they level, keeping arcane repertoires small, prized, and hard-won.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.schools_of_magic: Arcane traditions - High Magic, Elementalism, Necromancy, and others - define spell lists and arts, mixable through the Adventurer's partial classes.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.slot_based_invento: Items are readied or stowed against Strength-based slot limits (readied slots equal half Strength), and drawing stowed gear costs a Main Action.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.exhaustion: System Strain caps how much magical healing and other invasive effects a body can absorb: it rises with each effect, is capped by Constitution, and recovers one point per restful day.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Physical, Evasion, and Mental saves are d20 rolls against a target that starts near 15, improves by one per level, and is reduced by paired attribute modifiers.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: The book prescribes flat XP awards per session or goal against fixed per-level costs, decoupling advancement from gold or body counts.

title: Castles & Crusades
resolution_core: SIEGE Engine: d20 + level + attribute modifier vs. Challenge Base 12 (prime) or 18 + Challenge Level

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: The SIEGE Engine resolves nearly everything as d20 + level + attribute modifier against a Challenge Class, unifying skills, saves, and class abilities in one roll.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.variable_tn: The Challenge Class equals a Challenge Base of 12 (if the attribute is prime) or 18 (if not) plus the Challenge Level of the task or opponent.
vector_explanations.character.skills.broad_approaches: There is no skill list: every task maps to one of the six attributes as a SIEGE check, with class abilities riding on specific attributes.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Thirteen AD&D-flavored classes - knight, ranger, illusionist, assassin, barbarian among them - each anchor a prime attribute and their own XP table.
vector_explanations.character.identity.ancestry_heritage: Races grant ability adjustments and abilities like dwarven deepvision, while humans compensate by choosing three prime attributes to the demi-humans' two.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Ability scores are rolled on 3d6, but the choice of primes rather than raw numbers does most of the mechanical lifting.
vector_explanations.character.identity.alignment: The nine-alignment grid governs class entry - paladins must be lawful good - and shapes divine magic and monster behavior.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit dice range from the wizard's d4 to the barbarian's d12, rolled each level and modified by Constitution.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Initiative is rolled fresh each round on a d10 modified by Dexterity, keeping combat order fluid from round to round.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.fire_and_forget_memorizati: Wizards and clerics prepare spells into daily slots in Vancian fashion, and casting expends the preparation until the next study or prayer.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.spellbook_study: Wizards and illusionists keep spellbooks, study them to prepare their daily magic, and grow in power by adding captured or scribed spells to the book.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_levels: Spells are graded 0 through 9th level with per-day slots from class tables, including 0-level cantrips and orisons at the bottom rung.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.spell_lists_by_class: Wizards, illusionists, clerics, and druids each cast from their own list, echoing AD&D's four-caster split.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: XP awards for defeated challenges are divided among the party and measured against each class's individual advancement table.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At 0 hit points a character falls unconscious and bleeds toward death at negative totals, dying outright when the negatives run too deep.
vector_explanations.stealth.vision.darkvision_senses: Races carry graded senses - dwarven deepvision, elven twilight vision - with listed ranges for seeing underground or in dim light.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.armor_types: Armor from padded to full plate adds to a base ascending AC of 10, priced and weighted in classic equipment lists.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.abstract_load_leve: Encumbrance sums Encumbrance Values against a Strength-derived rating, pushing characters through burdened categories that slow movement.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.theater_of_mind: Combat runs gridless by default with movement rates in feet and Castle Keeper adjudication, though it ports easily onto a battlemat.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.wandering_monster: Random encounter tables in the companion Monsters & Treasure volume drive classic wandering-monster checks during travel and dungeon delves.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Saving throws are SIEGE attribute checks - dragon breath tests Dexterity, poison tests Constitution - so primes double as your best defenses.
vector_explanations.stealth.action.hide: The rogue's Hide and Move Silently are Dexterity-based SIEGE checks against observers' Challenge Levels, granting unseen movement and ambush attacks.
